title: CLI command reorganization + new fab update command (Rec#10 + Rec#11)
description: Improve CLI command discoverability and add a missing update lifecycle command: (1) remove hidden flag from config command, (2) move hooks as a fab config subcommand (fab config hooks), (3) remove standalone bootstrap entry point (functionality already inside init), (4) create new fab update command that re-runs MCP host install + hooks phases without recreating fabric files. Together these close the post-upgrade workflow gap and surface previously hidden commands.

changes: Remove hidden: true from config command registration
Remove standalone bootstrap command entry (if registered separately from init)
Add hooks subcommand: fab config hooks — exposing the hooks management functionality
Keep existing config show/set/get behavior unchanged
Create new update command that re-runs the MCP host install phase from init (update claude/cursor config, update mcp server registration)
Re-run hooks install phase (update pre-commit and post-merge hooks)
Compare current config against template and patch only changed fields (diff-and-apply, not overwrite)
Skip file creation steps (do not re-create .fabric/, AGENTS.md, etc.)
Output summary of what was updated

implementation: Read packages/cli/src/commands/index.ts to map all 11 registered commands and identify bootstrap and hidden config registrations
Read packages/cli/src/commands/config.ts to understand existing subcommand structure and add hooks subcommand
Read packages/cli/src/commands/init.ts to identify the MCP install phase and hooks phase logic to reuse in update.ts
Extract reusable installMcpHost() and installHooks() functions from init.ts (or call them directly from update.ts)
Create packages/cli/src/commands/update.ts implementing the update command using extracted functions
Remove hidden flag from config in index.ts; remove bootstrap registration if present
Update CLI help text and test that fab --help shows config and update commands

reference.pattern: Existing init command phases (MCP install + hooks install)
reference.examples: Extract the MCP install and hooks phases from init.ts as standalone functions; call them from both init and update

rationale.chosen_approach: Bundle CLI reorg + new update command as one task since both touch command registration in index.ts
alternatives_considered: Separate tasks for reorg and update command (double the index.ts modifications)
Implement update as an alias for 'init --skip-files' flag (less explicit, harder to document)
decision_factors: index.ts is the shared registration file for both changes — combined edit is cleaner
fab update as a first-class command improves discoverability vs a flag on init
rationale.tradeoffs: Extracting init phases into reusable functions is a mild refactor; low risk since init behavior is unchanged

risks.description: Removing hidden: true from config command may surface subcommands that are not fully polished for public consumption
risks.mitigation: Review config subcommands before removing hidden flag; ensure all show reasonable help text
risks.fallback: Mark individual subcommands as hidden if needed while making parent config visible

risks.description: fab update diff-and-apply logic for MCP host config may be complex if config format varies by host (Claude vs Cursor)
risks.mitigation: Start with full-overwrite of the fab section in each host config (not user-defined sections); add diff logic as follow-up
risks.fallback: fab update with --force flag for clean overwrite

criteria: fab config hooks --help shows hooks management options
fab update runs without creating new fabric files (no new AGENTS.md, .fabric/ created)
fab update updates MCP host config entry and git hooks
Bootstrap command no longer appears as standalone top-level command (if it was previously registered)
convergence.verification: Run fab --help and confirm config + update are visible; run fab update in a test project and confirm only MCP config + hooks updated; run fab config hooks --help
convergence.definition_of_done: Users upgrading fabric can run fab update to refresh their MCP host integration without re-running full init

unit: installMcpHost: applies updated MCP server config without touching user-defined sections
installHooks: overwrites pre-commit and post-merge with current templates
manual_checks: In a project with fabric initialized, run fab update — confirm MCP config updated, hooks updated, fabric files untouched
Run fab --help — confirm config and update are listed
Run fab config hooks — confirm hooks subcommand is accessible
success_metrics: fab --help shows >= 2 more visible commands than before (config + update)
fab update completes in < 5 seconds on a typical project

commit.message_template: feat(cli): add fab update command + surface config command + hooks as config subcommand
